title: allow mount via --device /dev/bsg/1:0:0:0 not work
body:
### Issue Description

archlinux podman 5.4.1 up to date.
podman run --device '/dev/bsg/1:0:0:0'   --device /devsr0  --device /dev/sg0-it localhost/archlinux_std_docker
#
Error: stat /dev/bsg/1: no such file or directory
#
we must wirte a --device with ":" to allow cd drive in podman coantiner to run.
on old podman syntax not work
podman run --device '/dev/bsg/1":"0":"0":"0' -it localhost/archlinux_std_docker
ich have problem with neu proton and winehq versions.
winehq not allow any more device in --device folder/2 and renames.
winehq will /dev/bsg/1:0:0:0 only. drm Safedisk and Securom protetion not work.

I have tested
podman run --device '/dev/bsg'   --device /devsr0  --device /dev/sg0-it localhost/archlinux_std_docker
#cdemu device
Error: failed to add /dev/bsg/10:0:0:0 device: invalid device specification: /dev/bsg/10:0:0:0:/dev/bsg/10:0:0:0
#

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

lsscsi -g
#
[1:0:0:0]    cd/dvd  ASUS     DRW-24D5MT       1.00  /dev/sr0 /dev/sg0
...
#

comments.body:
Tahnks you.
I found a other way.
